sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 32-year-old male, presents with a 2-week history of low back pain that started after lifting a box of books.
He describes the pain as achy and sharp in his low back, with radiation down his left leg.
The pain has worsened over time, but he has not experienced numbness, tingling, or weakness in his legs.
He denies loss of control of bladder or bowels.
He has tried taking ibuprofen and Tylenol, which sometimes helps.
He has not discussed surgery with anyone previously.
Past medical history: The patient's father has a history of back pain.
The patient has a history of playing soccer, but no other relevant medical history is mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: The patient takes ibuprofen and Tylenol as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 130/70, respiratory rate 18, regular heart rate and rhythm, clear lungs bilaterally, and normal temperature (not mentioned).
Physical exam: The patient has midline tenderness at the L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
He has pain with lumbar flexion, and a positive straight leg raise test.

Test Results: MRI results show a disk desiccation, a disk bulge with paracentral disk herniation resulting in moderate right neuroforaminal stenosis, consistent with a herniated disk with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has lumbar back pain at the L4-L5 disk space due to a herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain at the L4-L5 disk space; Herniated disk with nerve impingement; Lumbar radiculopathy.
Plan:
I prescribed a spinal injection with a strong NSAID and a corticosteroid to be administered under fluoroscopy.
I will schedule the procedure for today.
The patient will receive paperwork with explanations of the risks and benefits of the procedure.
I will answer any further questions the patient may have.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for the spinal injection procedure today.
